skill.name: skill-creator
skill.description: Guides agents through creating or updating AI skills with clear metadata, reusable resources, validation, and packaging steps.
skill.summary: Create and refine AI skills with structured guidance, helper scripts, validation, and packaging support.
skill.author: 92Bilal26

security_audit.summary: AI review dismissed the static weak-cryptography, network, and shell-backtick alerts as false positives from Apache license text, Markdown examples, and Python string templates. The skill does include helper scripts that create files and recursively package user-selected directories, so publication is reasonable with a filesystem warning and review of path handling.

medium_findings.title: Unvalidated Output Path Construction
medium_findings.description: The initializer builds the skill directory from a user-provided base path and skill name, then writes SKILL.md and resource files under that computed directory. The main function documents naming rules but does not enforce them before using the arguments, so untrusted input could create files outside the intended location.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The script clearly writes files to a path derived from command-line arguments. Risk depends on whether callers pass untrusted names or paths, so this is a medium-confidence filesystem issue rather than confirmed malicious behavior.

medium_findings.title: Recursive Packaging May Include Unintended Files
medium_findings.description: The packaging script recursively adds every file under the selected skill directory to a zip archive. This is legitimate packaging behavior, but it can disclose hidden files, local notes, or secrets if a user points it at a directory with unintended contents.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The recursive file inclusion is explicit and has no visible exclusion list. The behavior is expected for a packager, but it creates a realistic accidental disclosure risk.

low_findings.title: Static Analyzer False Positives in Documentation and License Text
low_findings.description: The reported weak-cryptography, hardcoded URL, shell-backtick, and reconnaissance alerts are explained by Apache license wording, Markdown inline code, fenced examples, and Python template strings. No evidence found of cryptographic operations, network requests, command execution APIs, or malicious reconnaissance in the reviewed files.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited locations are prose, Markdown examples, or deterministic validation code. The reviewed scripts use pathlib, zipfile, regex validation, and file writes, with no subprocess, eval, network client, or cryptographic API usage found.

dangerous_patterns.title: User-Controlled Filesystem Writes
dangerous_patterns.description: The initializer derives an output directory from command-line input and writes multiple files there. Validate skill names and constrain output paths before using this script in automated workflows.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The writes are direct and path-derived from inputs. The impact is limited by local execution context and user intent.

dangerous_patterns.title: Recursive Archive Creation
dangerous_patterns.description: The packager walks the entire selected skill directory and writes each file into a zip archive. Use a clean skill directory and exclude private files before packaging.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The recursive archive behavior is explicit. The risk is accidental disclosure, not evidence of malicious intent.

content.value_statement: Teams need repeatable ways to capture domain workflows for AI agents. This skill guides Claude, Codex, and Claude Code through skill design, scaffolding, validation, and packaging.
actual_capabilities: Explains the required structure for a skill folder and SKILL.md file.
Guides users through defining triggers, workflows, references, scripts, and assets.
Provides an initializer script that creates a starter skill with example resource folders.
Provides a validator for basic frontmatter and naming checks.
Provides a packaging script that validates a skill and creates a distributable zip file.
Encourages iterative testing and cleanup of unused generated resources.
limitations: The validator only performs basic structure and frontmatter checks.
The helper scripts do not enforce all documented naming rules before writing files.
The packager includes all files under the selected skill directory unless users clean it first.
The guidance is general and does not include domain-specific examples for every skill type.

use_cases.title: Create a New Team Skill
use_cases.description: Define a repeatable workflow and scaffold the files needed for an internal AI skill.

use_cases.title: Standardize Agent Instructions
use_cases.description: Convert scattered process notes into a concise SKILL.md with supporting references and assets.

use_cases.title: Package a Skill for Review
use_cases.description: Validate a skill folder and create a zip archive for testing or distribution.

prompt_templates.title: Start a Basic Skill
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner request for a new skill
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a new skill for handling support ticket triage. Ask only the key questions needed before scaffolding it.

prompt_templates.title: Refine Skill Instructions
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this skill and make the instructions concise, trigger-focused, and easy for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code to follow.

prompt_templates.title: Add Reusable Resources
prompt_templates.prompt: Analyze these example user requests and decide which scripts, references, or assets should be bundled in the skill.

prompt_templates.title: Prepare for Marketplace Review
prompt_templates.prompt: Validate this skill folder, identify files that should not be packaged, and prepare it for marketplace submission.

output_examples.input: Create a skill for monthly finance reporting.
output: A proposed skill name and trigger description.
A concise workflow outline for recurring report generation.
Suggested reference files for metrics definitions and reporting rules.

output_examples.input: Package my completed skill for sharing.
output: A validation result with any required fixes.
A warning to remove private files before packaging.
A distributable archive path after validation succeeds.

output_examples.input: Improve this skill so agents use it reliably.
output: A shorter SKILL.md workflow with resource references.
A list of unused example files to delete.

best_practices: Keep SKILL.md concise and move detailed material into referenced files.
Delete unused example scripts, references, and assets before packaging.
Validate names, paths, and frontmatter before sharing a skill archive.
anti_patterns: Do not package a working directory that may contain credentials or private notes.
Do not add broad instructions that trigger the skill for unrelated requests.
Do not keep generated placeholder resources that do not support the final skill.

faq.question: What does this skill help create?
faq.answer: It helps create AI skills with clear instructions, reusable resources, validation, and packaging steps.

faq.question: Does it work with Claude Code and Codex?
faq.answer: Yes. The concepts apply to Claude, Claude Code, and Codex style skill workflows.

faq.question: Does it generate a complete production skill automatically?
faq.answer: No. It scaffolds and guides the work, but users must customize the generated content.

faq.question: Why are helper scripts included?
faq.answer: They provide repeatable initialization, validation, and packaging steps for skill authors.

faq.question: What should users check before packaging?
faq.answer: Users should remove private files, unused placeholders, temporary notes, and credentials from the skill directory.

faq.question: Is this skill safe to publish?
faq.answer: It appears publishable with a filesystem warning because helper scripts write files and create archives.
